verified_result.description: DealCloud provides documented REST APIs for authentication, user management, schema, data, backups, publications, and relationship intelligence, plus a DealCloud MCP Server in client preview starting July 2026.
verified_result.auth_notes: Docs describe enabling a user API key in the DealCloud profile, and using OAuth2 Client Credentials to obtain bearer tokens for API requests. API access requires the user to belong to a group with the API capability enabled; otherwise contact an org admin or DealCloud Customer Success Manager.
verified_result.gating_reason: API access requires a user in DealCloud with API permissions enabled via Admin > User Management. If the user cannot see Admin, they must contact their organization's admin or DealCloud Customer Success Manager for assistance.

api_surface.breadth: Comprehensive documented REST APIs across authentication, user management, schema, data, backups, publications, relationship intelligence, files, reports, and more; Swagger/OpenAPI is available per DealCloud site.
api_surface.mcp_notes: DealCloud documents an MCP server, but it is marked as 'client preview starting July 2026', so availability may be limited.

verified_result.rate_limit_notes: Endpoint-specific limits are documented: OAuth token endpoint 300/minute; User Management 5/second; Data 5/second; Schema 5/second; RelIntel 5/second; Publications 1000/second; eMarketing 1000/second. Responses include X-Rate-Limit, X-Rate-Limit-Remaining, and X-Rate-Limit-Reset headers, and actual response headers are authoritative.
verified_result.notable_signals: Public docs explicitly say this site provides a comprehensive overview of DealCloud REST APIs and note each DealCloud site exposes an interactive Swagger page plus a Swagger/OpenAPI spec. Tokens expire after 900 seconds. MCP documentation is present.

verification_notes: Used only the provided fetched text. Classified access tier as admin_approval_required because API capability must be enabled in Admin > User Management and some users must contact an org admin/CSM for assistance. Marked buildability ready_today because public documentation, auth flow, endpoints, and rate limits are documented; while the MCP server is only in client preview, the REST API itself appears available.

api_surface.mcp_notes: No evidence of an MCP server in the fetched text.

Payment API-specific limit returns response=3 and response_code=301 ('Rate limit exceeded'); system-wide rate limit may return HTTP 429. Guidance: wait before retrying, reduce simultaneous connections, check credentials, contact support.
verified_result.notable_signals: Docs explicitly mention OpenAPI availability via llms.txt, OAuth 2.0 auth, sandbox endpoint for sale API, webhook signing/verification, and broad payment/account/reporting capabilities.
verified_result.human_review_reason: The app-specific Paygent Connect URL provided no content (HTTP status 0). Verification relies entirely on generic NMI documentation and cannot confirm whether 'Paygent Connect' specifically exposes the same API surface, access model, or branding relationship without human review.

verification_notes: Used only fetched evidence. Because the Paygent-specific source failed, I mapped capabilities from NMI docs referenced by the hint '(NMI-powered)'. This supports existence of an OAuth2-authenticated REST API with payment and webhook functionality, but app-specific applicability remains unconfirmed, so human review is required.

verified_result.description: Agentic document platform for AI teams, offering APIs for parsing, classification, extraction, splitting, editing, and pipelines for document processing at enterprise scale.
verified_result.auth_notes: REST API uses Bearer token via Authorization header with REDUCTO_API_KEY. Docs also describe browser-based approval/login for the Reducto MCP server (`uvx mcp-server-reducto --login`) and account signup in Studio to create API keys.
verified_result.gating_reason: Pricing page shows Standard plan is Free with first 15K credits included and 'Get started'; quickstart says sign up for a free account and create a new API key.

api_surface.breadth: Document-processing API includes upload, parse, extract, split, classify, edit, pipeline, async variants, job management, webhooks, SDKs for Python/Node/Go, and support for 30+ file types.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Docs explicitly list a Reducto MCP Server and quickstart includes install/auth instructions plus available tools such as parse_document, extract_data, split_document, classify_document, edit_document, and upload_file.

verified_result.rate_limit_notes: Docs state edge rate limits of 1,000 requests/sec sustained per API key for all endpoints, and 200 requests/sec sustained for GET /job/{job_id}. 429s are retried automatically by SDKs with exponential backoff. Separate concurrency throttle may queue parallel parse work instead of rejecting.
verified_result.notable_signals: Homepage says 'Try for free', 3B+ pages processed, 99.9%+ uptime, SOC2 and HIPAA compliance, deploy in customer environment, and enterprise SLAs. Docs mention SaaS, hybrid VPC, full VPC, or air-gapped on-prem deployments, async processing/webhooks, and native SDKs.

verification_notes: Grounded only in provided fetched text. Auth classified as token because docs explicitly require 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' / REDUCTO_API_KEY. Included 'other' because MCP login uses browser approval flow distinct from direct API auth. API surface marked REST; docs mention SDKs and MCP but no GraphQL evidence. Access tier set self_serve_free because free account, free credits, and self-serve API key creation are explicitly documented, despite higher tiers requiring contact sales.

verified_result.description: GitHub provides both REST and GraphQL APIs for creating integrations, retrieving data, and automating workflows across many GitHub resources.
verified_result.auth_notes: Docs state REST API authentication can use personal access tokens, tokens generated by a GitHub App, the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN in GitHub Actions, and OAuth tokens via OAuth apps. Some REST endpoints for GitHub Apps and OAuth apps require basic authentication using client ID as username and client secret as password. Username/password authentication is not supported.
verified_result.gating_reason: Fetched docs are for GitHub Docs 'Version: Free, Pro, & Team' and describe unauthenticated public-data access plus authenticated access via tokens, with no contact-sales or admin-approval requirement shown in the evidence.

api_surface.breadth: Extensive documented API surface: REST docs show 199 articles/endpoints categories across many resources; separate GraphQL docs provide schema reference and guides.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No MCP server or MCP-specific documentation is mentioned in the fetched evidence.

verified_result.rate_limit_notes: REST: unauthenticated public-data requests are 60 requests/hour; authenticated users generally 5,000 requests/hour, with higher limits for some Enterprise Cloud contexts. GitHub App installations use at least 5,000 requests/hour and can scale up to 12,500, or 15,000 on Enterprise Cloud. GITHUB_TOKEN is 1,000 requests/hour per repository (15,000 for enterprise resources). Secondary limits include max 100 concurrent requests and point-based per-minute controls. GraphQL: generally 5,000 points/hour per user or installation, 10,000 in some Enterprise Cloud cases, with separate secondary limits and node/query limits.
verified_result.notable_signals: REST API is versioned and fully described in an OpenAPI-compliant document. GraphQL offers flexible queries and separate schema/reference docs. Docs explicitly recommend using GitHub Apps over OAuth apps in many cases, and recommend webhooks instead of polling to avoid rate limits.

verification_notes: Verified directly from fetched GitHub Docs pages. REST home explicitly says 'Create integrations, retrieve data, and automate your workflows with the GitHub REST API' and mentions OpenAPI description. GraphQL home explicitly documents a separate GraphQL API. Authentication page supports token-based auth, OAuth app tokens, GitHub App tokens, GITHUB_TOKEN, and some basic-auth cases; username/password unsupported. Rate-limit pages provide concrete REST and GraphQL limits. Access tier inferred from docs availability and unauthenticated public-data support; no stronger gating evidence was present.

verified_result.description: Shopify provides developer tooling and APIs to build ecommerce apps, storefronts, and agentic commerce experiences that integrate with Shopify admin, online store, checkout, and related surfaces.
verified_result.auth_notes: Evidence shows agent authentication via client ID/client secret exchanged for a bearer access token at https://api.shopify.com/auth/access_token. The build docs also state apps authenticate with Shopify and manage access scopes, but the fetched evidence does not include a dedicated app-auth page confirming OAuth details, so OAuth2 is inferred from the authentication wording and token flow and should be reviewed by a human.
verified_result.gating_reason: Fetched docs say generating API credentials requires access to Shopify's Dev Dashboard, and app creation/management is done in Dev Dashboard.

api_surface.breadth: Broad API surface across GraphQL Admin API, Storefront API, Shopify Functions, Events, Webhooks, ShopifyQL, customer accounts, payments-related APIs, and agent/storefront MCP surfaces.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Docs explicitly mention Shopify MCP servers for agentic commerce, a Dev MCP server, and Storefront MCP.

verified_result.rate_limit_notes: Shopify documents rate limits. GraphQL Admin API uses calculated query cost with standard 100 points/second, Advanced 200, Plus 1000, enterprise 2000; single query max cost 1000. Storefront API has no fixed request-per-minute limit for real buyers, but automated traffic/bots are rate-limited. Input arrays max 250; pagination limited to 25,000 objects; 429 throttles and 430 security rejection may occur.
verified_result.notable_signals: Strong developer platform signals: Shopify CLI for apps, Dev Dashboard, App Store distribution, extensive app surfaces, webhooks/events, Shopify Functions, and explicit agentic commerce/UCP tutorials using Shopify MCP servers.
verified_result.human_review_reason: Fetched evidence is strong on APIs, MCP, token-based auth, and rate limits, but it does not directly show the main app authentication mechanism details or whether self-serve signup vs partner/admin approval is required for all API access. OAuth2 classification and access-tier classification therefore need human confirmation.

verification_notes: Verified only from fetched text. Evidence supports broad build surface and MCP existence. Authentication evidence explicitly shows client_credentials to bearer token for agents and mentions API keys/client secret. Build docs mention authentication and scopes but do not expose full app auth flow in fetched text, so auth methods and access tier retain some uncertainty.

verified_result.description: Stripe provides a REST API for payments, billing, accounts, payouts, webhooks, financial connections, tax, treasury, issuing, and many other fintech resources, with JSON responses and form-encoded request bodies.
verified_result.auth_notes: Stripe states the API uses API keys to authenticate requests. Example curl uses HTTP basic auth syntax with the API key as the username and a trailing colon. Docs describe restricted, secret, publishable, and organization API keys.
verified_result.gating_reason: Docs state that immediately after creating a Stripe account, you can use your account in testing environments/sandbox, and a sample test API key is included in examples.

api_surface.breadth: Very broad REST surface with many resource families listed in the API reference, including charges, customers, payment intents, checkout, billing, connect, payouts, treasury, tax, issuing, webhooks, and more.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Stripe documentation navigation includes 'Model Context Protocol' and 'Agent skills', indicating MCP-related documentation/features exist, but the fetched evidence does not confirm a standalone public MCP server endpoint.

verified_result.rate_limit_notes: Stripe documents global API limits of 100 requests/second live mode and 25 requests/second sandbox, plus endpoint-specific limits. 429 responses indicate rate limiting; docs recommend exponential backoff and note a Stripe-Rate-Limited-Reason header.
verified_result.notable_signals: Has official client libraries (Ruby, Python, PHP, Java, Node.js, Go, .NET); explicit sandbox/test mode; extensive changelog/versioning; many API resources; recommends restricted API keys and supports access policies.

verification_notes: Verified from fetched docs only. REST and authentication are explicit in API reference. Access tier inferred as self-serve free for sandbox/testing because docs say account can be used immediately in testing environments and sample test API key is included in examples; production/live payments may require account setup, but API experimentation is self-serve. Auth method includes api_key and basic because curl example uses basic-auth-style username:key syntax with the API key, though primary mechanism is API-key-based authentication. MCP existence marked true only because Stripe docs explicitly include 'Model Context Protocol' in navigation; evidence is insufficient to characterize implementation beyond existence of related documentation.

verified_result.description: Google Ads provides a programmatic interface for managing Google Ads accounts and campaigns, including automated account management, custom reporting, ad management based on inventory, and Smart Bidding strategy management.
verified_result.auth_notes: The docs explicitly include 'Authentication & authorization', 'OAuth 2.0 internals', user authentication workflows, and a 'login-customer-id' request header for API calls. The access model page also states users or service accounts can be given access to Google Ads accounts.
verified_result.gating_reason: Fetched pages describe authentication and access models but do not show self-serve API enrollment or pricing. Evidence is insufficient to confirm whether API access is immediately self-serve; the provided text does not explicitly state the signup gate.

api_surface.breadth: Extensive API surface for account management, campaigns, ads, budgets, bidding, reporting, audiences, conversions, keyword planning, experiments, and more.
api_surface.mcp_notes: The introduction page explicitly lists 'Google Ads MCP server' under the developer toolkit.

verified_result.blocker: The fetched evidence does not explicitly state the API access/enrollment gating requirements, so a human must confirm whether a developer token, account approval, or paid Google Ads account is required before production use.
verified_result.rate_limit_notes: The introduction page navigation references 'API limits and quotas', 'System limits', and 'Rate limits', but the fetched excerpts do not include actual numeric limits.
verified_result.notable_signals: Official client libraries are referenced. The docs explicitly mention REST API sections, OAuth 2.0, user/service account access concepts, webhook support, Google Ads scripts, and an official Google Ads MCP server.
verified_result.human_review_reason: The fetched evidence confirms API existence, REST docs, OAuth-based auth, and broad capabilities, but it does not explicitly state the API access approval/enrollment process or whether a developer token/payment/admin approval is required. Access tier and exact gating therefore need human confirmation.

verification_notes: Used only the provided fetched text. Marked auth as OAuth2 plus other because the evidence explicitly mentions OAuth 2.0 and also mentions service accounts/access model, but the excerpt does not fully detail service-account token mechanics. Marked API type as REST because the introduction page includes a REST API section; no GraphQL evidence found. Marked for human review because the provided excerpts do not explicitly state access gating or enrollment requirements.

verified_result.description: Notion provides a REST API for workspace content and collaboration data, including pages, databases, views, comments, files, search, and users; it also offers webhooks and a hosted Notion MCP server for AI tools.
verified_result.auth_notes: Public connections use OAuth 2.0. Internal connections use a static API token. Personal access tokens use a static bearer token/user-scoped token.
verified_result.gating_reason: Docs state developers can create personal access tokens and public/internal connections in the Developer portal, and note you can create and use a public connection without listing it on the Marketplace.

api_surface.breadth: REST API covers pages, databases/data sources, views, comments, file uploads, search, users, webhooks, and related objects/endpoints.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Docs include a Notion MCP section describing Notion MCP as a hosted server that connects AI tools to a Notion workspace via OAuth.

verified_result.rate_limit_notes: The API enforces per-connection limits averaging 3 requests/second with some bursts, plus per-workspace limits. Exceeding limits returns HTTP 429 with rate_limited; docs say to handle 429/529 and respect Retry-After.
verified_result.notable_signals: Webhooks are supported for real-time events. Public connections can be used without Marketplace listing, though Marketplace listing requires security review. OAuth token exchange returns access_token and refresh_token. Hosted MCP server exists for AI assistants.

verification_notes: Used only fetched Notion developer docs. Auth methods mapped from docs: OAuth 2.0 for public connections; static API token / bearer token for internal connections and PATs. Access tier set to self_serve_free because docs describe free personal workspace/PAT path and self-serve developer portal creation; however exact pricing plan availability for all features is not exhaustively detailed in the excerpts. API surface classified as REST based on explicit REST wording. MCP existence confirmed from Notion MCP overview.

verified_result.description: Zendesk is an AI-first customer and employee service platform for ticketing, help centers, messaging, live chat, phone, and contact center workflows, with integrations and APIs.
verified_result.auth_notes: Developer docs state API requests can be authorized using email address plus API token, or with an OAuth access token. Existing customers using basic authentication can continue for now, though Zendesk recommends API tokens or OAuth. Global OAuth tokens are required for apps/integrations distributed to multiple Zendesk customers.
verified_result.gating_reason: Main site and pricing page both advertise a 14-day free trial with no credit card required; paid plans also exist, and some enterprise plans require contacting sales.

api_surface.breadth: Public JSON REST API with Support/Help Center APIs and many documented endpoints; docs mention prebuilt integrations and APIs for custom workflows/data connections.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Fetched evidence only shows third-party GitHub repository search results for Zendesk MCP servers; no first-party Zendesk MCP product/documentation is shown.

verified_result.rate_limit_notes: Zendesk documents plan-based Support/Help Center API limits of 200/400/400/700/2500 requests per minute depending on Suite plan, 429 responses with Retry-After on limit exceed, ticketing-specific headers, endpoint-specific limits, jobs limit of 30 concurrent queued/running jobs, app limits, and account-wide limit of 100,000 requests/minute.
verified_result.notable_signals: Main site says 'Integrate Zendesk with your existing systems through prebuilt integrations and APIs' and highlights 1,800+ apps/integrations. Pricing page confirms self-serve purchase/trial for lower tiers and sales-assisted enterprise. Developer docs specify SSL/TLS 1.2+, JSON API, and multiple auth modes.

verification_notes: Used only fetched page text. Category is supported by product/pricing language around customer service, employee service, ticketing, help center, contact center. API surface is verified as REST/JSON from developer docs. Auth methods verified from security/auth page. Access tier chosen as self_serve_trial because site explicitly offers 14-day free trial; enterprise plan contact-sales noted in gating reason. MCP existence marked true only in the sense that public MCP servers exist in GitHub results, but notes clarify this is third-party and not first-party Zendesk evidence.
